mobile_ap.summary: Qualcomm is providing Samsung with an exclusive upgraded Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 variant featuring enhanced AI performance for the Galaxy Z Fold 8 lineup. Apple expanded its six-year partnership with Broadcom through 2031, announcing a new commitment to design and produce billions of custom silicon components and wireless connectivity technologies.

hpc_datacenter.summary: Nvidia expanded its Beersheba R&D center in Israel threefold to support AI chip demand, while Samsung opened an R&D center in Vietnam amid reports of potential layoffs and talent poaching. Apple is investing over €1 billion in its Munich silicon design center, and SK Hynix is aggressively recruiting specialized personnel in 54 roles focused on HBM and AI manufacturing to compete for semiconductor talent amid industry-wide shortages affecting Micron, Samsung, and TSMC's US factory expansions.

custom_soc.summary: China's leading AI developers DeepSeek and Zhipu AI are advancing custom AI inference chip development to reduce reliance on US suppliers, while startups like Etched are challenging Nvidia's dominance with specialized AI inference solutions. Arteris is collaborating with imec's IC-Link unit on reusable chiplet designs for AI and HPC, and a Philips ASIC design team has spun out as ICwaves, reflecting broader industry momentum toward in-house silicon and generative AI-assisted chip design tools.

foundry.summary: Rapidus is targeting 2nm foundry process pricing at or below TSMC's levels to compete in the advanced node market, while Samsung Foundry and Cadence deepened their 2nm and 3D-IC collaboration to address surging AI infrastructure demand. TSMC is expanding photonic integrated circuit (PIC) capacity 30-fold over three years and maintains pricing power with 72% foundry market share, while Intel is developing its 14A2 node with dual-side power technology to challenge TSMC and Samsung.

packaging.summary: TSMC is expanding CoWoS advanced packaging capacity to meet demand from high-performance AI chips, while ASE Technology continues riding the AI chip packaging boom with robust market position. SEALSQ and GlobalFoundries partnered to embed post-quantum security into the semiconductor packaging stack, and India is leveraging ₹64,000 crore in OSAT (outsourced semiconductor assembly and test) investments to accelerate domestic manufacturing as the US faces a projected 157,000-worker semiconductor talent shortfall by 2030.
